rag_rules: Fix unresolved property references by ensuring all referenced property definitions and constants exist in the AADL model or its dependencies.
Resolve type errors in AGREE expressions by aligning helper declarations with the correct scalar types (int, real, bool).
Remove invalid AGREE clauses that reference undefined identifiers or have mismatched types rather than adding missing architecture.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all identifiers are visible within the same annex scope or properly declared as helpers.
Validate that property associations and terms in AADL models reference valid definitions present in the model or configured packages.
Use only visible AADL features or locally declared helpers in AGREE contracts to prevent resolution failures.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks within valid component type or implementation scopes.
Type mismatches in AGREE expressions (e.g., <error> types) indicate unresolved references or incorrect helper declarations.
Property references in AGREE must resolve to valid definitions in the AADL model's package scope.
anti_patterns: Do not add new property definitions or constants to AGREE contracts if they are not already defined in the AADL model.
Avoid writing AGREE guarantees or assumptions that depend on implementation-only subcomponents or inaccessible features.
Do not attempt to fix type errors by introducing new architecture; instead, correct the root cause of the unresolved reference.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references requiring matching `with` clauses
Component type vs implementation scope placement rules
AGREE annex block syntax and structure
